node.statement: (Remark: Obstruction to nonlinear extensions) The BP argument fundamentally relies on linearity: the perturbation x + tD preserves constraints exactly (not just to first order), so the entire line segment to the boundary of V^+ is feasible. For nonlinear constraints, a feasible point can lie in the interior of a high-dimensional face with no feasible path to the boundary (e.g. interior shell counterexample). Linearity is essential.
